title: ranunculus neocuneatus
intro: Ranunculus neocuneatus, also known as the creeping buttercup, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and rounded leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce pain and swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Ranunculus neocuneatus include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antimicrobial activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or consumed orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
